segments.text: Guys, was Pokémon Go used by the CIA to spy on us? Cuz it turns out while we were playing Pokémon Go, we were
building out the most detailed map of the world without even realizing. And now robots are using it for building a digital twin of the real world. What is
really going on? Follow me and I'll explain everything. So, Niantic, the company behind Pokémon Go, added AR mapping in 2020. The game asked us to scan parks, [music] streets, monuments,
all as part of field research. But while you and I were playing, [music] Niantech was building a data gold mine. They literally got 30 billion real world
images scanned from every angle in every weather condition by millions of people worldwide. No wonder so many countries suspected this was being used for espionage. [music]
Now, nobody knows about that for sure,
but here's what we do know. Using that data, Niantic has built a visual positioning system, which is way more precise than satellite GPS. [music] And
Niantic got 100 million people to build it for free. And now, Niantic Spatial AI company has partnered with Coco Robotics. So, the delivery robots are
actually [music] navigating streets using your Pokémon scans, and they'll collect even more real-time data with every delivery. It's interesting [music]
and kind of scary to see how our Pokémon walks are powering the future of robotics. Share this with someone who was obsessed with catching
